phases.title: Alert delivery pipeline
phases.verdict: PASS (static; live provisioning + INV6 drill pending)

followUps.note: Operational-only (infra consent boundary): kind=authored, not done — nothing touches the repo (mechanics §5); the deliverable is the operational runbook in the ledger + its static verification record. USER must, per the ledger runbook: (1) create a Discord webhook and a Slack incoming webhook (URLs owned by user, kept OUT of the repo — INV11); (2) create ONE Grafana Cloud contact point (alert-dual-channel) holding BOTH a Discord integration and a Slack integration — Grafana fires all integrations in a contact point on every notification; (3) set the notification policy's Default policy contact point to alert-dual-channel (no nested routes) so every firing reaches both channels. This realization deviates from the plan's literal 'two contact points' — INV6 (both channels every firing) governs; do NOT use one-default + one match-all nested child, which redirects to the child and delivers Slack-only, failing INV6. This is redundancy, NOT Grafana failover; (4) run the INV6 drill — contact-point Test on alert-dual-channel (fires both integrations; bypasses routing), then one real drill-fired scratch rule whose single firing lands in Discord AND Slack through the policy; record the outcome. CW-native alarms (billing, EC2 auto-recovery) stay SNS→email — NOT rerouted here.

phases.title: Seoul replica silent-zero rule
phases.verdict: PASS (static; live CW-datasource provisioning + rule creation + INV6 through-policy drill pending, due 2026-07-27)

followUps.note: Operational-only (infra consent boundary): kind=authored, not done — nothing touches the repo (mechanics §5); the deliverable is the operational runbook in the ledger + its static verification record. DEADLINE 2026-07-27 (postmortem action item, handoff.md:138) — the ~30s cross-Pacific read outage went undetected because the Seoul RDS replica sat at 0 connections with nothing alerting. USER must, per the ledger runbook: (1) provision read-only AWS credentials for CloudWatch (IAM principal with ONLY the read-only permission set Grafana's CloudWatch-datasource docs specify; keep access key/role ARN + account id OUT of the repo — INV11); (2) create the Grafana Cloud CloudWatch datasource (Seoul region, the read-only creds); (3) create alert rule S on that datasource — namespace AWS/RDS, metric DatabaseConnections, dimension DBInstanceIdentifier=<Seoul replica instance id>, statistic Maximum, period 1 minute, condition IS EQUAL TO 0, pending period (for) 15m, No-Data handling as a SEPARATE state (this rule is literal-zero-scoped, NOT absent-metric); (4) route it to the phase-07 Default notification policy → alert-dual-channel so a firing reaches Discord AND Slack (redundancy, not failover); (5) run the drill — force rule S to fire once (drive replica to genuine 0, OR temporary trivially-true condition preserving routing, then revert) and confirm the SAME firing lands in Discord AND Slack via the policy; revert and record. This ONE drill also CLOSES phase 07's deferred INV6 through-policy leg (phase-07 verification.md named 'the Seoul replica rule once it exists') — run one drill, not a separate scratch rule. Independent of remote_write (can ship ahead of phase 09).

phases.title: Grafana-managed alert rules
phases.verdict: PASS (static; live rule creation + routing consent-gated, and rule-evaluation drills INV4/INV5/INV9/INV10 + G-argo firing half of INV8 deferred to step-3 remote_write)

followUps.note: Operational-only (infra consent boundary): kind=authored, not done — nothing touches the repo (plan/phase-09.md Files: none in-repo; mechanics §5). Deliverable = the operational runbook in phases/09-grafana-alert-rules/ledger.md + its static verification record. USER must, per the ledger runbook, create SEVEN Grafana-managed alert rules on the Grafana Cloud HOSTED-PROMETHEUS datasource (remote_write target, NOT CloudWatch) and route each to the phase-07 Default notification policy → alert-dual-channel (Discord primary + Slack fallback; NO lone match-all nested child — INV6 one-channel trap): (1) #7 Node-not-Ready kube_node_status_condition{condition=Ready,status=true}==0 for 15m; (2) #8 Backend-DaemonSet-unready kube_daemonset_status_number_ready{daemonset=backend}==0 for 5m; (3) #9 Container-waiting kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason{reason=~CrashLoopBackOff|ImagePullBackOff}==1 for 10m; (4) G-argo argocd_app_info OutOfSync/Degraded for 30m (CONFIRM exact metric+label spelling against live application-controller :8082 /metrics first); (5) G-eso ExternalSecret Ready==False sustained (CONFIRM against live ESO :8080 /metrics first); (6) G-etcd (time()-kube_etcd_snapshot_creation_timestamp_seconds)>(1.5*<snapshot_interval_seconds>), substitute the live k3s snapshot interval (INV11 — never commit it). Set each of these six rules' No-Data handling to a NON-paging state (Keep Last State / OK) — pre-step-3 the series are absent and must not page. (7) M per-cluster staleness meta absent_over_time(up{cluster=oregon}[10m])==1 + seoul twin — SEQUENCE M's creation WITH step-3 remote_write (an absent-detector fires on the pre-wiring absence). DEFERRED to step-3 remote_write wiring (BLOCKED-A, out of this task's Target): the rule-evaluation drills — INV4 (#7 fires after 15m on a stopped k3s agent), INV5 (surge 1→2→1 zero firings), INV9 (G-etcd fires past 1.5× interval), INV10 (M fires from surviving vantage on blocked egress), and the G-argo firing half of INV8. Grafana-managed rules evaluate on remote_written data, so no drill can fire until step-3 delivers it. INV6 contact-point delivery was drill-defined in phase 07/08, not re-opened here.

BLOCKED-B: cert-expiry (probe_ssl_earliest_cert_expiry, rider on §F blackbox) and clock-skew (node_timex_offset_seconds, rider on §E node_exporter) alerts are DEFERRED to handoff step-2 with their exporters — blackbox + node_exporter are NOT deployed (handoff step-2), so no metric seam exists to author these against in this phase. Excluded from phase-09 scope.
